docs.title: Welcome to GigOne Documentation
docs.text: GigOne is a next-generation AI companion designed specifically for India's gig economy workers (Uber, Swiggy, Rapido, etc.). It acts as a smart digital assistant that helps workers track earnings, log shifts, and receive personalized operational advice.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• 🗣️ Voice-First Interface: Interact with Gigi naturally using Hinglish (Hindi + English) voice commands.
  • 🌦️ Context-Aware Intelligence: Real-time integration with Weather and Traffic APIs for smarter suggestions.
  • 💰 Earnings Analytics: Consolidated view of income across multiple platforms.
  • 🚀 Modern Tech Stack: Powered by React, Node.js, MongoDB, and state-of-the-art AI models (Gemini + Groq).

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
• System Overview: Understand the high-level design and data flow.
  • Tech Stack: Explore the technologies powering the platform.
  • Setup Guide: Step-by-step instructions to run the project locally.

docs.title: API Documentation
docs.text:
• Server API: detailed reference for backend endpoints and services.
  • Client App: Overview of the frontend architecture and component library.

docs.title: Project Documentation Log
docs.text:
This file contains detailed documentation for files we work on. As changes are made or new features are developed, entries will be added here to explain:

  • What the file does
  • Important code concepts/patterns used
  • Any related testing code

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: This service generates Gigi's conversational replies. It takes the user's spoken text (already transcribed), their sentiment analysis, recent chat history, and optionally weather/traffic context — then asks Gemini to produce a short, empathetic Hinglish reply.

docs.title: Main Code Concepts
docs.text: Concept Why @google/generative-ai SDK Connects to Google Gemini (gemini-2.0-flash model) for text generation Prompt Engineering A detailed system prompt defines Gigi's personality (warm, Hinglish, 2-3 sentences, empathetic) Context window management Only the last 6 conversation turns are included via .slice(-6) to stay within token limits Optional context injection Weather & traffic data are conditionally appended to the prompt so Gigi can reference real-world conditions

docs.title: Key Function
docs.text:
generateReply(userText, sentiment, recentMessages, context);
// → Returns a string: Gigi's reply

Flow:

  1. Build a history block from recentMessages (last 6 turns)
  2. Build a context block from weather and traffic (if available)
  3. Construct a prompt with personality rules + mood + context + history + current user message
  4. Call model.generateContent(prompt)
  5. Return the cleaned text

docs.title: Testing Code
docs.text: • None yet. Will be tested end-to-end via POST /api/gigi/chat.

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: MongoDB schema that stores all voice conversation messages per user. Uses a sub-document pattern — each message has a role (user or assistant), text, and optional sentiment data.

docs.title: Main Code Concepts
docs.text: Concept Why Sub-document schema (messageSchema) Each message is its own mini-schema with role, text, sentiment, and auto timestamps Parent schema (conversationSchema) One document per user with a messages[] array — all turns are appended here mongoose.Schema.Types.ObjectId with ref: "User" Links each conversation to a user via MongoDB reference index: true on userId Speeds up queries when fetching a user's conversation timestamps: true Auto-adds createdAt and updatedAt on both message and conversation level

docs.title: Key Structure
docs.text:
Conversation {
  userId     → ObjectId (ref User)
  messages[] → [
    { role, text, sentiment: { mood, score, summary, suggestion }, createdAt }
  ]
  createdAt, updatedAt
}

docs.title: Testing Code
docs.text: • None yet. Will be tested when the chat controller saves conversations.

docs.title: API Testing Guide
docs.text:
Use these sample requests to test the API. You can use Postman, Thunder Client (VS Code extension), or curl.

Note: Your MongoDB must be connected for these to work. If you see ReplicaSetNoPrimary errors, check your internet connection or MongoDB Atlas whitelist.

docs.title: 1. Sign Up (Register)
docs.text:
POST http://localhost:5000/api/auth/register
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "name": "TestUser",
  "email": "test@test.com",
  "password": "test1234"
}

Expected Response:

{
  "token": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIs...",
  "user": { "id": "...", "name": "Raju Kumar", "role": "user" }
}

📋 Copy the token value — you need it for all other requests!

docs.title: 2. Sign In (Login)
docs.text:
POST http://localhost:5000/api/auth/login
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "email": "test@test.com",
  "password": "test1234"
}

Expected Response: Same as above (token + user).

docs.title: 3. Start a Check-in Conversation
docs.text:
POST http://localhost:5000/api/chat/start
Authorization: Bearer <paste-your-token-here>

Expected Response:

{
  "conversationId": "65f...",
  "step": "mood",
  "reply": "Hey! Aaj kaisa raha din? Kuch batao! 😊"
}

📋 Copy the conversationId — you need it for replies!

docs.title: 4. Reply with Text (Testing Flow)
docs.text: Send these one at a time, in order. Each one advances the conversation step.

docs.title: Step 1 → mood (tell about your day)
docs.text:
POST http://localhost:5000/api/chat/reply-text
Authorization: Bearer <your-token>
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "conversationId": "<paste-conversationId>",
  "text": "Aaj bohot thak gaya yaar, bahut traffic tha"
}

docs.title: Step 2 → platform (which app you worked on)
docs.text:
POST http://localhost:5000/api/chat/reply-text
Authorization: Bearer <your-token>
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "conversationId": "<same-conversationId>",
  "text": "Uber pe kaam kiya aaj"
}

docs.title: Step 3 → earnings (how much you earned)
docs.text:
POST http://localhost:5000/api/chat/reply-text
Authorization: Bearer <your-token>
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "conversationId": "<same-conversationId>",
  "text": "Aaj barah sau mila, 1200 rupees"
}

docs.title: Step 4 → hours (how long you worked)
docs.text:
POST http://localhost:5000/api/chat/reply-text
Authorization: Bearer <your-token>
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "conversationId": "<same-conversationId>",
  "text": "Paanch ghante kaam kiya aaj"
}

docs.title: Final Response (summary + done)
docs.text:
After Step 4, the AI gives a summary with suggestion. Response will have:

{
  "step": "done",
  "isComplete": true,
  "extractedData": { "platform": "uber", "earnings": 1200, "hours": 5 }
}

docs.title: 5. Get Weather & Traffic Context
docs.text:
GET http://localhost:5000/api/chat/context?lat=12.9716&lon=77.5946
Authorization: Bearer <your-token>

(Uses Bangalore coordinates as example)

docs.title: Sign Up
docs.text: curl -X POST http://localhost:5000/api/auth/register -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"name":"Raju Kumar","email":"raju@test.com","password":"test1234"}'

docs.title: Start Chat
docs.text: curl -X POST http://localhost:5000/api/chat/start -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN_HERE"

docs.title: Send Text Reply
docs.text: curl -X POST http://localhost:5000/api/chat/reply-text -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN_HERE" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"conversationId":"YOUR_CONVO_ID","text":"Aaj bohot thak gaya"}'

docs.title: 1. Speech-to-Text (STT)
docs.text:
How it works: - User holds the mic and speaks. - The app records the voice. - When the mic is released, the audio is sent to our backend. - The backend sends the audio to the Groq API (using the Whisper model). - Groq turns the audio into text instantly.

Code Files to Show: - server/services/groqService.js (Where the Groq Whisper model is called) - server/controllers/chatController.js (Where the backend handles the audio upload)

Why we chose Groq (Whisper): - It is extremely fast. Users don't have to wait long. - High accuracy. It understands what users say clearly. - Cost-effective. It gives high performance at a low cost.

docs.title: 2. Chatbot Brain (LLM)
docs.text:
How it works: - Once we have the text, we send it to Google Gemini. - We also send: the past chat history, current weather, and local traffic. - Gemini reads all this and does three things at once: 1. Writes a short, friendly reply. 2. Guesses the user's mood (happy, tired, etc.). 3. Extracts important data (like today's earnings).

Code Files to Show: - server/services/conversationService.js (Where the chatbot prompts and step-by-step conversation logic are) - server/services/geminiService.js (Where the Gemini API is configured)

Why we chose Gemini (Flash-Lite): - It is fast. The Flash-Lite model gives very quick answers. - Does multiple jobs at once. It can chat and pull out data (like earnings) in a single step. - Great free tier. Helps keep the project costs low.

docs.title: 3. Text-to-Speech (TTS)
docs.text:
How it works: - We get the text reply from Gemini. - The app sends it to a custom Edge TTS server (built with Python). - Edge TTS converts the text into a real-sounding voice file. - The app plays the voice file to the user. - If the server fails, the app uses the browser's built-in voice instead.

Code Files to Show: - client/src/pages/user/DashBoard.jsx (Where the frontend plays the incoming voice) - openai-edge-tts/app/server.py (The Python server that creates the voice)

Why we chose Edge TTS: - Free and realistic. It offers high-quality Indian-accent voices for free. - Better user experience. Built-in browser voices often sound robotic, Edge sounds natural.

docs.title: 4. Architecture Diagram (How Parts Connect)
docs.text:
graph TD
    UserUI(Frontend Dashboard) -->|1. Sends Audio| Backend(Node.js Server)
    Backend -->|2. Sends Audio| Groq(Groq API / Whisper)
    Groq -->|3. Returns Text| Backend
    Backend -->|4. Sends Text + Weather| Gemini(Google Gemini API)
    Gemini -->|5. Returns Reply & Data| Backend
    Backend -->|6. Sends Reply Text| UserUI
    UserUI -->|7. Asks for Audio| EdgeTTS(Python Edge TTS Server)
    EdgeTTS -->|8. Returns Voice file| UserUI

What the diagram means: - The mobile/web app talks to our backend server. - The backend uses Groq to understand the voice. - The backend uses Gemini to think of a reply. - Finally, the app uses an Edge TTS server to speak the reply out loud.

docs.title: 5. Sequence Diagram (Step-by-Step Flow)
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant App as Frontend
    participant Server as Backend
    participant AI as Gemini & Groq
    participant TTS as Edge TTS

    App->>Server: Send voice recording
    Server->>AI: Convert voice to text (Groq)
    AI-->>Server: Return text
    Server->>AI: Think of a reply (Gemini)
    AI-->>Server: Return text reply & user mood
    Server-->>App: Send text reply to screen
    App->>TTS: Make audio for the text
    TTS-->>App: Send audio file
    App->>App: Play audio out loud

What the sequence means: - First: The app sends the user's voice to the server. - Second: The server uses AI APIs to get the text, figure out the reply, and extract data. - Third: The server gives the written reply back to the app. - Fourth: The app turns that written reply into voice using the TTS server and plays it.

docs.title: GigOne Architecture Overview
docs.text: GigOne is a MERN-stack application designed to empower gig economy workers by providing an AI companion (Gigi), detailed earnings analytics, and operational support.

docs.title: Core Components
docs.text:
The system is divided into two main domains:

  1. Client Application (Frontend):

    • Built with React and Vite.
    • Features a responsive dashboard with real-time updates.
    • Integrates voice interaction via the Web Audio API and Edge TTS.
    • Uses Shadcn/UI and Tailwind CSS for a modern, accessible interface.

  2. Server API (Backend):

    • Node.js and Express REST API.
    • MongoDB for persistent storage of users, work logs, and earnings.
    • AI Services Layer: Orchestrates interactions with Gemini (Sentiment/NLG), Groq (Whisper/ASR), OpenWeather (Context), and TomTom (Traffic).

docs.title: Conversational AI Loop
docs.text:
1. User Input: Worker speaks into the app. Audio is captured via useVoiceRecorder.
  2. Transcription: Audio is sent to the server and transcribed using Groq's Whisper model.
  3. Context Gathering: Server fetches real-time weather and traffic data based on user location.
  4. Unified AI Processing: Transcription + Context is sent to Gemini 1.5 Flash.
    • Sentiment Analysis: Determines mood (e.g., "stressed", "happy").
    • Data Extraction: Identifies entities like earnings (1200), platform (Uber), or hours (5).
    • Response Generation: Crafts a personalized, empathetic Hinglish response.
  5. Synthesis & Playback: The text response is converted to speech (Edge TTS) and played back to the user.

docs.title: Security
docs.text:
• Authentication: Dual-strategy using JWT (JSON Web Tokens).
    • Local Strategy (Email/Password) via Bcrypt.
    • OAuth 2.0 Strategy (Google) via Passport.js.
  • Authorization: Middleware ensures only authenticated users can access personal data.

docs.title: Frontend (Client)
docs.text: Technology Purpose React Component-based UI library. Vite Next-generation frontend tooling and bundler. Tailwind CSS Utility-first CSS framework for rapid styling. Shadcn/UI Accessible, reusable component primitives (Radix UI based). Axios Promise-based HTTP client for API requests. React Router Declarative routing for SPAs.

docs.title: Backend (Server)
docs.text: Technology Purpose Node.js JavaScript runtime environment. Express Fast, unopinionated web framework for Node.js. MongoDB NoSQL database for flexible data schemas. Mongoose ODM (Object Data Modeling) library for MongoDB. Passport.js Authentication middleware (Google OAuth Strategy). Multer Middleware for handling multipart/form-data (audio uploads).

docs.title: AI & External Services
docs.text: Service Function Google Gemini 1.5 Flash Brain: Sentiment analysis, natural language generation, entity extraction. Groq (Whisper) Ears: Ultra-fast speech-to-text transcription. OpenAI Edge TTS Voice: High-quality, localized text-to-speech synthesis (e.g., en-IN-NeerjaNeural). OpenWeatherMap Context: Real-time weather conditions and forecasts. TomTom Traffic Context: Real-time traffic flow and congestion data.

docs.title: Client Application Overview
docs.text: The client is a Single Page Application (SPA) built with React, serving as the user interface for workers and administrators.

docs.title: Directory Structure
docs.text:
client/src/
├── components/
│   └── ui/            # Reusable shadcn/ui components (Button, Card, Input)
├── hooks/             # Custom React hooks (useVoiceRecorder)
├── lib/               # Utilities and API clients
├── pages/
│   ├── admin/         # Admin dashboard views
│   ├── auth/          # SignIn and SignUp pages
│   └── user/          # Worker dashboard and feature pages
├── services/          # Domain-specific API wrappers (chatApi)
├── App.jsx            # Main router configuration
└── main.jsx           # Entry point

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Glassmorphism Design: A modern, dark-themed UI with translucent cards and neon accents.
  • Voice-First Interface: Primary interaction model for the AI companion.
  • Real-time Dashboard: Live clock, dynamic weather badges, and immediate AI feedback.

docs.title: UI Components
docs.text: The project uses a component library based on Shadcn/UI, which leverages Radix UI for accessibility and Tailwind CSS for styling.

docs.title: Button
docs.text:
• Path: components/ui/button.jsx
  • Variants: default (primary indigo), outline, ghost, link.
  • Usage: Used for all interactive triggers.

docs.title: Card
docs.text:
• Path: components/ui/card.jsx
  • Sub-components: CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardContent, CardFooter.
  • Usage: The main container for dashboard widgets (Earnings, AI Chat, Stats).

docs.title: Input & Label
docs.text:
• Path: components/ui/input.jsx, components/ui/label.jsx
  • Usage: Building blocks for the Authentication forms.

docs.title: Tabs
docs.text:
• Path: components/ui/tabs.jsx
  • Usage: Switching between "User" and "Admin" login modes on the Sign In page.

docs.title: useVoiceRecorder
docs.text:
Path: hooks/useVoiceRecorder.js

Manages the browser's MediaRecorder API to capture audio.

  • State: isRecording (boolean).
  • Methods:
    • startRecording(): Requests mic permission and begins capturing chunks.
    • stopRecording(): Stops capture, compiles chunks into a Blob, and returns it.

docs.title: chatApi
docs.text:
Path: services/chatApi.js

A dedicated wrapper for chat-related network requests.

  • startSession(): POST /api/chat/start
  • sendAudioReply(blob): POST /api/chat/reply (multipart/form-data)
  • getContext(lat, lon): GET /api/chat/context
  • synthesizeSpeech(text): POST to local Edge TTS service.

docs.title: api (Axios Instance)
docs.text:
Path: lib/api.js

The global Axios client configured with: - Base URL: From VITE_API_URL. - Interceptors: Automatically injects the Authorization: Bearer <token> header from LocalStorage into every request.

docs.title: cn
docs.text:
Path: lib/utils.js

A helper for conditionally merging Tailwind classes. Combines clsx and tailwind-merge.

docs.title: Sign In (pages/auth/SignIn.jsx)
docs.text:
• Route: /signin
  • Features:
    • Tabbed interface for User vs. Admin login.
    • Local email/password form.
    • "Continue with Google" OAuth button.

docs.title: Sign Up (pages/auth/SignUp.jsx)
docs.text:
• Route: /signup
  • Features: Registration form for new users.

docs.title: Dashboard Home (pages/user/DashBoard.jsx)
docs.text:
• Route: /user/dashboard
  • Key Sections:
    • Top Bar: Live clock, Weather badge, Status indicator.
    • AI Companion: Central avatar, chat bubble history, "Hold to Speak" mic button.
    • Overview Panel: Weekly earnings bar chart (CSS-only), recent work logs list.

docs.title: Feature Pages (Skeleton)
docs.text:
• Earnings: /user/earnings - Detailed financial reports.
  • Work Logs: /user/work-logs - Shift history.
  • Weekly Report: /user/weekly-report - Aggregated insights.
  • Shift Planner: /user/shift-planner - Schedule optimization.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Node.js v18+
  • MongoDB (Local or Atlas)
  • API Keys:
    • Google Gemini
    • Groq Cloud
    • OpenWeatherMap
    • TomTom
    • Google OAuth Client Credentials

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
1. Clone the repository: bash git clone https://github.com/your-repo/gigone.git cd GigOne

  2. Install Server Dependencies: bash cd server npm install

  3. Install Client Dependencies: bash cd ../client npm install

  4. Python TTS Service (Optional): If using the local Edge TTS server: bash cd ../openai-edge-tts pip install -r requirements.txt python app/server.py

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Create a .env file in server/:

PORT=5000
MONGO_URI=mongodb://localhost:27017/gigone
JWT_SECRET=your_jwt_secret
GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=...
GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=...
GEMINI_API_KEY=...
GROQ_API_KEY=...
OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=...
TOMTOM_API_KEY=...

docs.title: Running the App
docs.text:
1. Start Backend: bash # In server/ npm run dev

  2. Start Frontend: bash # In client/ npm run dev

Access the app at http://localhost:5173.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
1. Sign Up: Create a new user via the form. Verify redirection to Dashboard.
  2. Login: Log out and log back in.
  3. Google Auth: Click "Google", verify consent screen, and callback redirection.

docs.title: AI Chat Flow
docs.text:
1. Greeting: Verify the AI greets you by name and mentions the weather.
  2. Voice Interaction:
    • Hold the mic button.
    • Speak a sentence (e.g., "I am feeling good, worked on Uber today").
    • Release button.
    • Verify:
      • "Processing..." state appears.
      • Transcription appears in the chat bubble.
      • Audio reply plays back automatically.
      • Conversation advances to the next logical step (e.g., asking for earnings).

docs.title: Automated Testing (Planned)
docs.text:
• Backend: Jest + Supertest for API endpoint validation.
  • Frontend: Vitest + React Testing Library for component rendering and interaction tests.
  • E2E: Playwright for full user journey testing.

docs.title: Debug Scripts
docs.text:
Located in server/:

  • node test-extract.js: Tests the Gemini JSON extraction logic in isolation.
  • node test-reply.js: Simulates a full HTTP conversation loop (Start Session -> Reply) to verify server stability.

docs.title: Server API Overview
docs.text: The GigOne server is a robust Express.js application that serves as the backend for the client application. It manages user authentication, data persistence, and the orchestration of AI services.

docs.title: Directory Structure
docs.text:
server/
├── config/         # Database and Passport configuration
├── controllers/    # Business logic for API endpoints
├── middleware/     # Auth and validation middleware
├── models/         # Mongoose schemas (User, WorkLog, Conversation)
├── routes/         # API route definitions
├── services/       # External API integrations (AI, Weather, Traffic)
└── index.js        # Entry point

docs.title: 1. Authentication (/api/auth)
docs.text: Handles user registration, login, and Google OAuth flows. Issues JWTs for session management.

docs.title: 2. Chat & AI (/api/chat)
docs.text: The heart of the companion experience. Manages voice/text inputs, context gathering, and the conversational state machine.

docs.title: 3. Data Management (/api/earnings, /api/worklogs)
docs.text: CRUD operations for worker's historical data, enabling analytics and reporting.

docs.title: Authentication API
docs.text: The authentication system uses a hybrid approach supporting both local email/password credentials and Google OAuth 2.0.

docs.title: Register User
docs.text:
POST /api/auth/register

Creates a new user account.

Body:

{
  "name": "John Doe",
  "email": "john@example.com",
  "password": "securePassword123"
}

Response: Returns a JWT token and user profile.

docs.title: Login
docs.text:
POST /api/auth/login

Authenticates an existing user.

Body:

{
  "email": "john@example.com",
  "password": "securePassword123"
}

docs.title: Google OAuth
docs.text:
GET /api/auth/google

Initiates the Google sign-in flow. Redirects to Google's consent screen.

GET /api/auth/google/callback

Callback URL handled by Passport.js. On success, redirects to the client application with the JWT token in the URL query parameters.

docs.title: authMiddleware
docs.text: Located in server/middleware/auth.js. - Intercepts requests to protected routes. - Verifies the Authorization: Bearer <token> header. - Decodes the JWT and attaches the user object (userId, role) to req. - Returns 401 Unauthorized if invalid.

docs.title: Chat & AI System
docs.text: The Chat API orchestrates the multimodal interaction between the worker and Gigi (the AI companion).

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text: The conversation is modeled as a state machine with the following steps: 1. Greeting 2. Mood Check 3. Platform Identification 4. Earnings Report 5. Hours Logged 6. Summary/Closing

docs.title: Start Session
docs.text:
POST /api/chat/start

Initializes a new conversation. - Returns: A personalized greeting based on the user's name and current weather. - Side Effect: Creates a new Conversation document in MongoDB.

docs.title: Reply (Voice)
docs.text:
POST /api/chat/reply

Processes a user's voice response.

Body (Multipart): - audio: The recorded audio file (WebM/WAV). - conversationId: ID of the active session. - lat, lon: (Optional) User's current location.

Process: 1. Transcribe: Audio -> Text via Groq (Whisper). 2. Context: Fetches Weather + Traffic. 3. Process Turn: Sends everything to Gemini. - Analyzes sentiment. - Extracts data (e.g., earnings amount) if relevant to the current step. - Generates the next AI response. 4. Update State: Advances the conversation step (e.g., from mood to platform). 5. Save: Updates the Conversation document.

docs.title: Get Context
docs.text:
GET /api/chat/context

Fetches standalone environmental data. - Query: ?lat=...&lon=... - Returns: Weather conditions and traffic congestion levels.

docs.title: conversationService.js
docs.text:
• processChatTurn: The core function that constructs the prompt for Gemini, including history and context.
  • STEP_CONFIG: Defines the goals and extraction targets for each conversation state.

docs.title: geminiService.js
docs.text: • Handles pure text analysis and sentiment scoring using Google's Generative AI SDK.

docs.title: groqService.js
docs.text: • Interfaces with Groq Cloud to perform ultra-low latency audio transcription.

docs.title: Data Management API
docs.text: These endpoints manage the core business data: earnings and work logs.

docs.title: Get Earnings
docs.text:
GET /api/earnings

Retrieves a history of earnings entries. - Sort: Descending by date. - Filter: Scoped to the authenticated user.

docs.title: Add Earning
docs.text:
POST /api/earnings

Records a new financial entry.

Body:

{
  "platform": "Uber",
  "amount": 1500,
  "hours": 8,
  "date": "2023-10-27T10:00:00Z"
}

docs.title: Get Work Logs
docs.text:
GET /api/worklogs

Retrieves historical shift data.

docs.title: Add Work Log
docs.text:
POST /api/worklogs

Logs a completed shift.

Body:

{
  "platform": "Swiggy",
  "hours": 5.5,
  "notes": "Heavy rain, high surge pricing today.",
  "date": "2023-10-27T14:30:00Z"
}

docs.title: EarningsEntry
docs.text:
• userId: ObjectId (Ref: User)
  • platform: Enum (Uber, Swiggy, Rapido, Other)
  • amount: Number
  • hours: Number
  • date: Date

docs.title: WorkLog
docs.text:
• userId: ObjectId (Ref: User)
  • platform: Enum
  • hours: Number
  • notes: String
  • date: Date

docs.title: External Services Integration
docs.text: The server integrates with several third-party APIs to provide intelligence and context.

docs.title: Gemini Service (services/geminiService.js)
docs.text:
• Provider: Google Vertex AI / Generative AI
  • Model: gemini-1.5-flash
  • Role: The "Brain". Handles NLG (Natural Language Generation), intent classification, and entity extraction.

docs.title: Groq Service (services/groqService.js)
docs.text:
• Provider: Groq Cloud
  • Model: whisper-large-v3
  • Role: The "Ears". Provides near-instant speech-to-text transcription.

docs.title: Weather Service (services/weatherService.js)
docs.text:
• Provider: OpenWeatherMap
  • Endpoints: Current Weather, 5-Day Forecast
  • Role: Provides atmospheric context. For example, if it's raining, Gigi might suggest wearing a raincoat or expecting surge pricing.

docs.title: Traffic Service (services/trafficService.js)
docs.text:
• Provider: TomTom Traffic API
  • Endpoint: Flow Segment Data
  • Role: Provides road congestion data. Helps Gigi advise on whether traffic conditions are favorable for driving.
